chapter_title.en: Chapter 30 – Bequeathing of a sword includes its sheath, and bequeathing a container included its contents

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/74)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/74)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى لِرَجُلٍ بِسَيْفٍ وَ كَانَ فِي جَفْنٍ وَ عَلَيْهِ حِلْيَةٌ فَقَالَ لَهُ الْوَرَثَةُ إِنَّمَا لَكَ النَّصْلُ وَ لَيْسَ لَكَ الْمَالُ قَالَ فَقَالَ لَا بَلِ السَّيْفُ بِمَا فِيهِ لَهُ

قَالَ فَقُلْتُ رَجُلٌ أَوْصَى لِرَجُلٍ بِصُنْدُوقٍ وَ كَانَ فِيهِ مَالٌ فَقَالَ الْوَرَثَةُ إِنَّمَا لَكَ الصُّنْدُوقُ وَ لَيْسَ لَكَ الْمَالُ قَالَ فَقَالَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) الصُّنْدُوقُ بِمَا فِيهِ لَهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Abu Jameela,

(It has been narrated) from Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a man who bequeathed a sword to a man, and it was in a sheath, and upon it were ornaments. So the inheritors said to him, ‘But rather, for you is the blade, and the wealth (ornaments) is not for you’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, but the sword with whatever it was it, is for him’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘So I said, ‘A man bequeathed a container and there used to be wealth in it. So the inheritors said, ‘But rather, for you is the container and the wealth is not for you’. So Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The container along with whatever is in it, is for him’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from abu Jamilah who has narrated the following: “I once asked al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who has made a will for a man about a sword which is in a cover (sword sheathing) with decorations on it. The heirs said that only the sword is for him and not the asset (the valuable decorations). He (the Imam) said, ‘The sword and that in which it is belongs to him.’ I then asked about the case of a man who makes a will about a box for a man. The heirs say that only the box without its contents belongs to him. ‘Not only the box but its contents also belong to him’, said abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam .”
